source: .claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scenario-meanings.md → quota-burndown (multi-turn rate-limit usage burndown in one interactive codex session)
notes: Three back-to-back substantive turns in one live codex Plus REPL session that approaches but never hits the quota cap. Unlike headless `codex exec` (single-shot, ends in process_exited), the interactive driver keeps the rollout session alive across all turns, so the daemon observes three full ready→working→ready arcs on the SAME rollout session_id, then process_exited only when the driver tears the tmux REPL down. The daemon sees the proc-PID pre-session born ready, then the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then per-turn working/ready flips. The first phase anchors to start UNPINNED and the turn phases pin same_session_as:session_birth so the transient proc-PID row can't steal identity. The burndown evidence the scenario validates lives in events.jsonl payloads, not the phase DSL (which has no metric form): codex emits a token_count event_msg per turn carrying rate_limits.primary.used_percent; extractCodexRateLimitWindow reads used_percent DIRECTLY into RateLimitWindow.UsedPercent with a per-event SampledAt, and ingestRateLimit stores the latest snapshot plus a sample-on-change RateLimitHistory. The user-observable signal: Windows[0].UsedPercent after turn N ≥ after turn N-1 (non-decreasing), and SampledAt advances each turn (snapshot re-read, not replayed stale). The fixture does NOT reach the cap (token-quota-exhausted is the separate sibling cell, gap:429-mock) — even a fractional rise validates the burndown path; if UsedPercent is numerically equal very early in a window, an advancing SampledAt is sufficient to confirm the snapshot is being re-read. Codex plan-mode token_count suppression (#322/#325) is a separate known caveat, not a daemon bug — the recipe uses non-plan turns where the canonical signal lives.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within ~2s of codex launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
First user prompt → working; turn 1 produces the first token_count carrying a rate-limit window with used percent P1 sampled at T1
First turn ends → back to ready (state reset between turns)
Second user prompt → working on the same session; turn 2 reports the window again with used percent P2 ≥ P1 sampled at T2 > T1 (non-decreasing burndown, fresh sample)
Second turn ends → back to ready
Third user prompt → working on the same session; turn 3 reports the window with used percent P3 ≥ P2 sampled at T3 > T2 (usage still non-decreasing, never reset to 0)
Third turn ends in ready; session never enters waiting and never hits the quota cap across the conversation
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
